title: A repair shop I visit
summary: Describe a small repair shop where things get fixed instead of thrown away.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a small repair shop you visit. Describe the shop, the person who works there, and what you bring to fix. Use present simple.
hintStarters: There is a small repair shop near…
The man who works there can fix…
I take my old things to him when…
sampleText: There is a small repair shop near the old market in Hanoi. The shop is dark and full of things. The man who works there is called Mr. Tuan. He is about sixty years old. He sits at a small work table by the door. Behind him there is a shelf full of parts. He can fix many things. He fixes radios, fans, small clocks, and bags with broken zippers. The shop smells of oil and metal. I take my old things to Mr. Tuan when they stop working. Last week I took him a fan from my kitchen. He looked at it for ten minutes, and he changed one small part. Now the fan works again, and the price was very cheap. He is a patient man and he never says a job is too hard. I do not like to throw away things that I can fix. People like Mr. Tuan help my things last for years.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: có mùi dầu và kim loại

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Using 'can' for ability
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'can' + động từ nguyên mẫu để nói về khả năng: 'He can fix many things', 'things that I can fix'. 'can' không thay đổi theo chủ ngữ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'can' + base verb expresses ability: 'He can fix many things', 'things that I can fix'. 'can' does not change with the subject.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for one finished event
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể về một việc đã xong tuần trước, dùng quá khứ đơn: 'I took him a fan', 'He looked at it', 'he changed one small part'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When telling about something finished last week, use the past simple: 'I took him a fan', 'He looked at it', 'he changed one small part'.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một tiệm sửa chữa nhỏ gần chợ cũ ở Hà Nội. Tiệm tối và đầy đồ. Người làm việc ở đó tên là chú Tuấn. Chú khoảng sáu mươi tuổi. Chú ngồi ở một cái bàn làm việc nhỏ cạnh cửa. Phía sau chú có một cái kệ đầy phụ tùng. Chú có thể sửa nhiều thứ. Chú sửa radio, quạt, đồng hồ nhỏ và túi xách bị hỏng khóa kéo. Tiệm có mùi dầu và kim loại. Tôi mang đồ cũ đến chú Tuấn khi chúng hỏng. Tuần trước tôi mang đến chú một cái quạt trong bếp. Chú nhìn nó trong mười phút và thay một phụ tùng nhỏ. Bây giờ cái quạt lại chạy, và giá rất rẻ. Chú là một người kiên nhẫn và không bao giờ nói một công việc quá khó. Tôi không thích vứt đi những thứ có thể sửa được. Những người như chú Tuấn giúp đồ của tôi dùng được nhiều năm.

options: Near the old market in Hanoi
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'There is a small repair shop near the old market in Hanoi'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the place mentioned in the first sentence.

mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He is about sixty years old'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find his age in the early sentences.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which item is NOT mentioned as something he fixes?
mcQuestions.explanation: Cars are not on the list. The text mentions radios, fans, small clocks, and bags with broken zippers.
mcQuestions.hint: Read the list of things he fixes and find the one that is missing.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the shop smell of?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The shop smells of oil and metal'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the two smells mentioned together.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer take to Mr. Tuan last week?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Last week I took him a fan from my kitchen'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the past simple sentence about last week.

options: He can to fix many things.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'can', use the base form of the verb without 'to' or -s.
mcQuestions.hint: 'can' is followed by the base form of the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did Mr. Tuan look at the fan?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He looked at it for ten minutes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the number of minutes mentioned.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the correct past simple of 'take'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'take' is irregular. Its past simple is 'took'.
mcQuestions.hint: Recall the irregular past form of 'take'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer think about throwing things away?
options: The writer likes to throw things away
The writer does not like to throw away things that can be fixed
The writer throws everything away once a year
The writer never owns broken things
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I do not like to throw away things that I can fix'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the negative sentence about throwing away.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is correct present simple negative?
options: He no says a job is too hard.
He don't says a job is too hard.
He never says a job is too hard.
He not say a job is too hard.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never' goes before the main verb: 'he never says'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the option with a correct adverb of frequency in the right place.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why is repairing things often better than buying new ones? What do we lose when we throw away everything that breaks?
